response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web and full-stack credentials, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ experience was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; his recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. He shows no exposure to IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, industrial protocols, or message-broker architectures at the depth required, making him a poor fit despite solid seniority.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and mentoring/workshop experience
Some systems-level background: low-latency async services (DLang) and past C/C++ work at National Instruments
concerns: Minimal and outdated professional C++/STL experience (last used ~2015)
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure; no MQTT or industrial protocol experience
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), not high-performance networked C++ systems
